event.title: UN Adds Israel and Russia to Conflict-Related Sexual Violence Blacklist
event.summary: The United Nations added Israel and Russia to its annual 'Conflict-Related Sexual Violence' blacklist for the first time, citing verified cases of sexual abuse by their forces. Israel responded by cutting ties with UN Secretary-General António Guterres and rejecting the allegations.

image.caption: United Nations Headquarters in New York City (file photo)
image.file_title: File:Headquarters of the United Nations, New York City, 20231001 1103 1006.jpg

sources.author_background: Emma Graham-Harrison is The Guardian's Chief Middle East correspondent who has reported extensively from conflict zones including Syria, Iraq, and Afghanistan, and won multiple awards for her coverage of human rights issues in the region.

claims.text: The UN added Israel to its Conflict-Related Sexual Violence blacklist for the first time.
claims.rationale: This fact is reported across outlets spanning left (Al Jazeera), center-left (The Guardian, Haaretz), center (BBC, Reuters), and center-right (Ynet, i24), making it a cross-spectrum agreed claim.

framing_variants.characterization: Framed as the UN taking action against Israel by adding it to a blacklist
Neutral factual reporting: 'Israel has been added to a UN blacklist of countries that commit sexual violence in warzones for the first time'
Framed as Israel being included on a 'notorious UN blacklist' alongside gang rape, sexual slavery and torture
Direct statement: 'The UN has added Israel and Russia to a blacklist for sexual violence in conflict'
Framed as Israel being 'blacklisted for sex crimes alongside Hamas'

claims.text: The UN added Russia to its Conflict-Related Sexual Violence blacklist for the first time.
claims.rationale: Reported by left (Al Jazeera), center-left (The Guardian), and center (BBC, Reuters) outlets, providing cross-spectrum corroboration.

framing_variants.characterization: Russian armed and security forces blacklisted for sexual violence against prisoners of war and civilian detainees during the war in Ukraine
Direct statement that UN added Russia to the blacklist for sexual violence in conflict

claims.text: The UN report lists 77 countries and armed groups.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian specifies the total number of countries and armed groups listed in the UN report.

framing_variants.characterization: Presented as context for the scope of the UN blacklist

claims.text: The UN verified 31 cases of conflict-related sexual violence perpetrated by Israeli forces against Palestinians from Gaza and the West Bank between 2023 and 2025.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) report the same figure of 31 verified cases, spanning different bias tiers.

framing_variants.characterization: Presented as UN verification of cases, with 13 in 2025 and 18 in the prior two years
Presented as verified sexual abuse of 31 Palestinian men, women and children between 2023 and 2025

claims.text: 13 of the 31 verified cases of Israeli sexual violence occurred in 2025 and 18 in the two prior years.
claims.rationale: Only BBC provides this temporal breakdown of the 31 verified cases.

framing_variants.characterization: Neutral factual reporting of temporal distribution of verified cases

claims.text: The 31 verified cases of Israeli sexual violence involved 14 men, 7 women, 9 boys, and 1 girl.
claims.rationale: Only BBC provides this demographic breakdown of verified victims.

framing_variants.characterization: Neutral factual reporting of victim demographics

claims.text: Violations verified by the UN included rape, gang rape, forced nudity, and violence to genitals committed by Israeli military, police, and prison service officers.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) document similar types of violations and perpetrator affiliations, spanning different bias tiers.

framing_variants.characterization: Violations included rape, gang rape, forced nudity, and violence to genitals by Israeli military, police and prison service

Includes more detailed enumeration: rape with objects, attempted rape, attacks on genitals, targeted shooting of genitals, touching of breasts and genitals, forced nudity and threats of rape; perpetrators included soldiers, prison officers and elite police counter-terrorism unit members

claims.text: The UN characterized the 31 verified cases as indicative of patterns rather than a comprehensive account, citing restrictions placed on UN investigators by Israel.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) report the UN's caveat about investigative restrictions limiting the scope of findings.

framing_variants.characterization: UN said its attempts to fully investigate had been obstructed by the Israeli government
UN said cases were 'indicative of incidents and patterns' rather than a comprehensive summary, because of restrictions on investigators

claims.text: Israel barred UN experts from detention centres, blocked travel to Gaza, and threatened Palestinian detainees if they reported abuse after release.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) report these specific obstruction measures cited by the UN.

framing_variants.characterization: Framed as obstruction of investigation and threats against detainees
Explicitly listed as obstruction measures including barring experts, blocking travel, and threatening detainees

claims.text: Israel has not allowed the International Committee of the Red Cross to visit detainees since October 2023.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian reports this specific restriction on ICRC access.

framing_variants.characterization: Presented as part of broader pattern of restrictions on independent oversight of detention

claims.text: Israel rejected the UN allegations and stated it submitted evidence, documents, and detailed responses to every claim.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) report Israel's denial and stated engagement with the process.

framing_variants.characterization: Israel said it rejected allegations of sexual abuse
Danon said Israel 'submitted evidence, documents, and detailed responses to every claim'

claims.text: Israel cut ties with UN Secretary-General António Guterres in response to the blacklisting, with the Israeli UN mission stating it would refuse contact with his office for as long as he serves.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) confirm Israel's diplomatic break with Guterres's office.

framing_variants.characterization: Israel stated it intended to break off relations and would refuse contact with Guterres's office as long as he serves
Danon said the country had 'cut ties' with Guterres in response to the blacklisting

claims.text: Israeli Ambassador to the UN Danny Danon accused Guterres of spreading antisemitic lies.
claims.rationale: Only BBC explicitly quotes Danon's accusation that Guterres spread antisemitic lies.

framing_variants.characterization: Reported as a factual accusation made by Danon

claims.text: A UN official stated they did not review evidence before blacklisting Israel.
claims.rationale: Only i24 News reports this claim about a UN official's statement regarding evidence review, framing it as an admission.

framing_variants.characterization: Framed as a UN official admitting 'not my job' to review evidence before the blacklisting decision

claims.text: Hamas was previously added to the UN Conflict-Related Sexual Violence report for the October 7 attacks on Israel and for abuse of hostages in Gaza.
claims.rationale: BBC (center), The Guardian (center-left), and i24 News (center-right) all confirm Hamas's prior listing, spanning different bias tiers.

framing_variants.characterization: Hamas was added to the previous report last year
UN had already added Hamas for the October 7 attacks and for abuse of hostages in Gaza
Framed as Israel being blacklisted 'alongside Hamas'

claims.text: 12 former hostages released from Gaza made allegations of sexual violence against Hamas captors, but the UN was unable to verify these reports because Israel denied UN bodies access to carry out investigations.
claims.rationale: Only BBC provides the specific figure of 12 hostages and the explanation that Israeli denial of access prevented UN verification of Hamas-related allegations.

framing_variants.characterization: Framed as a notable consequence of Israeli obstruction affecting even investigation of Hamas-related allegations

claims.text: The UN verified 310 cases of conflict-related sexual violence by Russian forces in Ukraine and Russian-occupied territories, including rape, gang rape, genital mutilation, and electric shocks applied to genitals.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) confirm the 310 figure and types of violence documented against Ukrainians.

framing_variants.characterization: 310 instances including rape and genital mutilation, affecting 280 men, 26 women and 4 girls
310 cases verified, with two-thirds involving multiple forms of sexual violence and more than half of survivors enduring repeated attacks

claims.text: The majority of Russian sexual violence victims verified by the UN were men, with 26 women and 4 girls also among the victims.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) report the same demographic breakdown of Russian abuse victims.

framing_variants.characterization: Stated as 280 men, 26 women and 4 girls
Most victims were men, with 26 women and four girls also abused

claims.text: Russia barred monitors from accessing prisoners of war and civilians in detention, obstructing the UN investigation.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian explicitly reports Russia barring monitors from accessing detainees as an obstruction measure.

framing_variants.characterization: Presented as parallel to Israeli obstruction, noted as a factor limiting investigation scope

claims.text: Russia has not publicly commented on the UN report.
claims.rationale: Only BBC notes that Russia made no public comment on the report.

framing_variants.characterization: Reported factually as an absence of response, contrasted with Ukraine's welcome of the report

claims.text: Ukraine's foreign minister Andrii Sybiha welcomed the UN report as 'a crucial step on the painful road to truth and accountability.'
claims.rationale: Only BBC reports Ukraine's foreign minister's welcoming statement about the report.

framing_variants.characterization: Reported as a direct quote from Sybiha

claims.text: Ukrainian forces also committed 31 verified incidents of conflict-related sexual violence since 2022, including beating of genitals, applying electric shocks and forced nudity, mostly before 2025.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian reports the specific number and types of Ukrainian force sexual violence incidents verified by the UN.

framing_variants.characterization: Presented alongside Ukrainian government's cooperation with monitors and steps to strengthen laws addressing sexual violence

claims.text: The Sde Teiman detention facility incident: five Israeli guards were caught on leaked CCTV footage appearing to sexually abuse a detainee from Gaza, a doctor found corroborating wounds, and charges against the guards were dropped by Israel's top military lawyer in March 2026.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) report the filmed incident, medical corroboration, and subsequent dropped charges.

framing_variants.characterization: Reported factually; charges dropped by top military lawyer in March of this year
Netanyahu described the alleged perpetrators as 'heroic' and the prosecution attempt as 'criminal'; the victim was never charged or tried and has since been released

claims.text: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu described the guards accused in the Sde Teiman case as 'heroic' and the prosecution attempt against them as 'criminal.'
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian reports Netanyahu's characterizations of the accused guards and the failed prosecution.

framing_variants.characterization: Framed as part of a broader pattern of impunity and political cover for alleged abusers

claims.text: The UN stated that a systemic lack of accountability for sexual violence by Israeli forces helped create a culture of impunity.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian explicitly quotes the UN's finding about a systemic lack of accountability creating a culture of impunity.

framing_variants.characterization: Presented as a direct UN finding

claims.text: Nearly nine in ten Israeli military investigations into crimes committed by soldiers since the start of the Gaza war were closed without finding fault or without resolution; out of 52 cases studied by Action on Armed Violence, only one resulted in a prison conviction.
claims.rationale: Only BBC reports these specific findings from the research organisation Action on Armed Violence.

framing_variants.characterization: Presented as context for systemic accountability failures

claims.text: The Israeli military stated it conducts examination and investigation processes for exceptional incidents where there is suspicion of a violation of law, and opens criminal investigations when there is reasonable suspicion of an offence in accordance with Israeli and international law.
claims.rationale: Only BBC reports the Israeli military's formal statement about its investigative procedures.

framing_variants.characterization: Reported as the military's official response to allegations

claims.text: Benjamin Netanyahu and Foreign Minister Gideon Sa'ar announced a defamation lawsuit against the New York Times over a report based on testimonies of 14 Palestinian sexual abuse survivors, calling it 'one of the most hideous and distorted lies ever published against the state of Israel.'
claims.rationale: Only BBC reports the Netanyahu-Sa'ar joint statement and defamation lawsuit against the New York Times.

framing_variants.characterization: Reported as the government's response to NYT investigative reporting on Palestinian sexual abuse testimonies

claims.text: Far-right national security minister Itamar Ben-Gvir caused a diplomatic crisis by publishing footage of Israeli security forces abusing international activists detained while trying to sail to Gaza with aid; at least 15 activists said they were sexually assaulted in custody, including at least one rape.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian reports the Ben-Gvir incident, the published footage, and the activists' accounts of sexual assault.

framing_variants.characterization: Presented as illustrating that forms of abuse used against Palestinian prisoners were also used against international activists; Ben-Gvir described as having 'boasted of a prison revolution'

claims.text: The UN Committee against Torture stated in November that it was deeply concerned about reports indicating a de facto state policy of organised and widespread torture and ill-treatment of Palestinian detainees in Israeli jails, which had gravely intensified after the October 7, 2023 Hamas-led attacks.
claims.rationale: Only BBC reports this specific prior statement by the UN Committee against Torture.

framing_variants.characterization: Presented as additional context demonstrating prior UN concern about Israeli detention conditions

claims.text: Guterres placed Israel on notice prior to the blacklisting, urging it to investigate allegations and improve detention conditions for Palestinians.
claims.rationale: Only BBC reports Guterres's prior 'notice' to Israel and his stated urging on detention conditions.

framing_variants.characterization: Presented as precedent to the formal blacklisting, paralleling how Guterres also warned Russia last year

claims.text: Details of the UN report were shared by Israeli diplomats at the UN before its official release, and the full report was subsequently posted online by the US news site PassBlue.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian reports that Israeli diplomats circulated the report pre-release and that PassBlue posted the full document online.

framing_variants.characterization: Presented as context for how the report became public ahead of its official release

claims.text: The UN report found that global conflict-related sexual violence rose sharply from 2024, was marked by extreme brutality, and overwhelmingly targeted women and girls; Israel and Russia diverge from the global trend by also targeting men.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian reports this global trend finding and the comparative note about Israel and Russia targeting men.

framing_variants.characterization: Presented as a global finding from the UN report providing context for the Israel and Russia listings

background.point: The UN's annual Conflict-Related Sexual Violence report lists parties to armed conflicts credibly suspected of committing or being responsible for patterns of sexual violence. Being listed — informally called a 'blacklist' — is a formal UN designation intended to pressure accountability.
Hamas was added to the UN Conflict-Related Sexual Violence report the previous year for sexual violence during the October 7, 2023 attacks on Israel and for abuse of hostages in Gaza.
The Gaza war began following Hamas-led attacks on Israel on October 7, 2023, which killed approximately 1,200 people and resulted in around 250 hostages being taken to Gaza.
Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine began in February 2022; Russia had been warned by Guterres about sexual violence the previous year before being formally listed in this report.

Rights groups and UN bodies have documented a pattern of restrictive and allegedly abusive conditions in Israeli detention facilities since October 2023, including Israel's refusal to grant ICRC access to detainees.

paragraphs.text: The United Nations added Israel and Russia to its annual Conflict-Related Sexual Violence blacklist for the first time, according to a report released in late May 2026. The listing was confirmed by outlets across the political spectrum, including Al Jazeera, BBC News, The Guardian, Haaretz, Reuters, Ynet News, and i24 News. Hamas was previously added to the UN report for sexual violence committed during the October 7 attacks on Israel and for abuse of hostages held in Gaza.

The UN verified 31 cases of conflict-related sexual violence perpetrated by Israeli forces against Palestinians from Gaza and the West Bank between 2023 and 2025. The documented violations included rape, gang rape, forced nudity, and violence to genitals, committed by Israeli military, police, and prison service officers. These findings were reported by both BBC News and The Guardian, drawing on center and center-left sources.

The UN characterized the 31 verified cases as indicative of patterns rather than a comprehensive account, citing restrictions Israel placed on UN investigators. Israel barred UN experts from detention centres, blocked travel to Gaza, and threatened Palestinian detainees if they reported abuse after release, according to the report. These investigative obstacles were documented by both BBC News and The Guardian.

For Russian forces in Ukraine and Russian-occupied territories, the UN verified 310 cases of conflict-related sexual violence, including rape, gang rape, genital mutilation, and electric shocks applied to genitals. The majority of Russian sexual violence victims verified by the UN were men, with 26 women and 4 girls also among the victims. Both BBC News and The Guardian reported these figures.

Israel rejected the UN allegations and stated it submitted evidence, documents, and detailed responses to every claim. In response to the blacklisting, Israel cut ties with UN Secretary-General António Guterres, with the Israeli UN mission stating it would refuse contact with his office for as long as he serves. This diplomatic break was confirmed by both BBC News and The Guardian.

One documented case involved the Sde Teiman detention facility, where five Israeli guards were caught on leaked CCTV footage appearing to sexually abuse a detainee from Gaza. A doctor found corroborating wounds on the victim. Charges against the guards were dropped by Israel's top military lawyer in March 2026. This incident and its outcome were reported by both BBC News and The Guardian.

BBC News reported that of the 31 verified Israeli cases, 13 occurred in 2025 and 18 in the two prior years. The victims included 14 men, 7 women, 9 boys, and 1 girl. The Guardian separately reported that Israel has not allowed the International Committee of the Red Cross to visit detainees since October 2023.

The Guardian reported that the UN stated a systemic lack of accountability for sexual violence by Israeli forces helped create a culture of impunity. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu described the guards accused in the Sde Teiman case as 'heroic' and the prosecution attempt against them as 'criminal,' according to The Guardian.

BBC News reported findings from the research organization Action on Armed Violence showing that nearly nine in ten Israeli military investigations into crimes committed by soldiers since the start of the Gaza war were closed without finding fault or without resolution. Out of 52 cases studied, only one resulted in a prison conviction. The Israeli military stated it conducts examination and investigation processes for exceptional incidents where there is suspicion of a violation of law, opening criminal investigations when there is reasonable suspicion of an offense in accordance with Israeli and international law.

The Guardian reported that far-right national security minister Itamar Ben-Gvir caused a diplomatic crisis by publishing footage of Israeli security forces abusing international activists detained while trying to sail to Gaza with aid. At least 15 activists said they were sexually assaulted in custody, including at least one rape. BBC News reported that the UN Committee against Torture stated in November that it was deeply concerned about reports indicating a de facto state policy of organized and widespread torture and ill-treatment of Palestinian detainees in Israeli jails, which had gravely intensified after the October 7, 2023 Hamas-led attacks.

The Guardian reported that the UN report lists 77 countries and armed groups. The report found that global conflict-related sexual violence rose sharply from 2024, was marked by extreme brutality, and overwhelmingly targeted women and girls; Israel and Russia diverge from the global trend by also targeting men.

BBC News reported that 12 former hostages released from Gaza made allegations of sexual violence against Hamas captors, but the UN was unable to verify these reports because Israel denied UN bodies access to carry out investigations. The Guardian reported that Russia barred monitors from accessing prisoners of war and civilians in detention, obstructing the UN investigation. BBC News noted that Russia made no public comment on the report.

BBC News reported that Ukrainian forces also committed 31 verified incidents of conflict-related sexual violence since 2022, including beating of genitals, applying electric shocks and forced nudity, mostly before 2025, according to The Guardian. BBC News reported that Ukraine's foreign minister Andrii Sybiha welcomed the UN report as 'a crucial step on the painful road to truth and accountability.'

Israeli Ambassador to the UN Danny Danon accused Guterres of spreading antisemitic lies, according to BBC News. The Israeli center-right outlet i24 News reported that a UN official stated they did not review evidence before blacklisting Israel. Benjamin Netanyahu and Foreign Minister Gideon Sa'ar announced a defamation lawsuit against the New York Times over a report based on testimonies of 14 Palestinian sexual abuse survivors, calling it 'one of the most hideous and distorted lies ever published against the state of Israel,' BBC News reported.

BBC News reported that Guterres placed Israel on notice prior to the blacklisting, urging it to investigate allegations and improve detention conditions for Palestinians. The Guardian reported that details of the UN report were shared by Israeli diplomats at the UN before its official release, and the full report was subsequently posted online by the US news site PassBlue.
